rules.description: First 1-2 sentences clearly define what the page is about
rules.fix: Rewrite your opening to clearly define your topic in the first 1-2 sentences. AI models extract definitions from page intros. Start with: '[Topic] is a [category] that [does what]'

rules.description: Definition is concise and extractable (under 40 words)
rules.fix: Make your opening definition concise and self-contained — AI models need to extract it as a standalone answer

rules.description: Primary entity/product is named explicitly in the first paragraph
rules.fix: Name your product/service/topic explicitly in the first paragraph — don't use pronouns or vague references

rules.description: Page has an FAQ section or Q&A format content
rules.fix: Add an FAQ section — AI models heavily pull from Q&A formatted content for direct answers

rules.description: FAQ section has FAQPage schema markup
rules.fix: Add FAQPage JSON-LD schema to your FAQ section — this is the #1 way to get into AI-generated answers

rules.description: Questions are in proper question format (start with who/what/when/where/why/how)
rules.fix: Format FAQ questions as natural questions starting with Who/What/When/Where/Why/How — matches how users ask AI

rules.description: FAQ answers are concise (40-200 words each)
rules.fix: Keep FAQ answers between 40-200 words — concise enough for AI to extract, detailed enough to be useful

rules.description: Page has JSON-LD structured data
rules.fix: Add JSON-LD structured data — this is the primary way AI systems understand page content type and structure

rules.description: JSON-LD uses specific type (not just WebPage)
rules.fix: Use a specific schema type (Article, Product, FAQ, HowTo) instead of generic WebPage — gives AI better context

rules.description: JSON-LD includes all recommended properties for its type
rules.fix: Fill in all recommended schema properties — more structured data = more ways for AI to reference your page

rules.description: Page has multiple complementary schema types
rules.fix: Add multiple schema types (e.g., Organization + FAQPage + BreadcrumbList) for comprehensive AI coverage

rules.description: Content includes specific numbers, statistics, or data points
rules.fix: Add specific statistics, data points, and numbers — AI models preferentially cite content with concrete data

rules.description: Content signals original research or unique data
rules.fix: Include original research, proprietary data, or unique findings — AI strongly prefers citing primary sources

rules.description: Content includes expert quotes or attributions
rules.fix: Add expert quotes with proper attribution — AI models cite content with authoritative sources

rules.description: Content provides clear definitions of key terms
rules.fix: Include clear definitions of key terms — AI models extract and cite definitional content

rules.description: Content includes comparison tables or vs analysis
rules.fix: Add comparison content ('X vs Y', comparison tables) — these are among the most-cited content types in AI answers

rules.description: Primary entity uses consistent naming throughout the page
rules.fix: Use consistent naming for your product/brand throughout the page — don't alternate between variations

rules.description: Primary entity is introduced by full name on first mention
rules.fix: Introduce your entity by its full, official name on first mention before using abbreviations

rules.description: Entity is placed in clear context (category, industry, function)
rules.fix: Provide category context for your entity (e.g., 'Sphinx, an SEO analysis tool' not just 'Sphinx')

rules.description: Page includes how-to or step-by-step content
rules.fix: Add step-by-step instructions or how-to content — AI models frequently pull procedural content for answers

rules.description: How-to content has HowTo schema markup
rules.fix: Add HowTo JSON-LD schema to your instructional content for direct AI extraction

rules.description: Content uses lists (ordered/unordered) for key points
rules.fix: Use bullet points and numbered lists for key information — AI models prefer structured, scannable content

rules.description: Content uses tables for comparative or structured data
rules.fix: Add tables for comparative data, pricing, features — AI models extract tabular information effectively

rules.description: Content directly answers questions in concise paragraphs
rules.fix: Write concise, direct answers to likely questions — start paragraphs with the answer, then elaborate

rules.description: Page has datePublished metadata
rules.fix: Add datePublished to your schema markup and visible on page — AI models prefer content with publication dates

rules.description: Page has dateModified metadata
rules.fix: Add dateModified to your schema markup — AI models strongly prefer recently updated content

rules.description: Publication or update date is visible on the page
rules.fix: Display the publication/update date visibly on your page — this is a trust signal for both users and AI

rules.description: Content was published or updated within the last 12 months
rules.fix: Update your content regularly and reflect the updated date — stale content loses AI visibility over time

rules.description: Page has author information
rules.fix: Add author name and credentials — AI models prioritize content from identified, credible authors

rules.description: Author has Person schema markup
rules.fix: Add Person schema for your author with name, jobTitle, and sameAs links to authoritative profiles

rules.description: Page links to an about/team page
rules.fix: Link to your about/team page — establishes organizational authority for AI citation

rules.description: Site has Organization schema
rules.fix: Add Organization JSON-LD schema with name, url, logo, description, and sameAs social links

rules.description: GPTBot (OpenAI) is not blocked in robots.txt
rules.fix: Allow GPTBot in robots.txt to appear in ChatGPT answers. Add: User-agent: GPTBot\nAllow: /

rules.description: ClaudeBot (Anthropic) is not blocked in robots.txt
rules.fix: Allow ClaudeBot in robots.txt to appear in Claude answers. Add: User-agent: ClaudeBot\nAllow: /

rules.description: PerplexityBot is not blocked in robots.txt
rules.fix: Allow PerplexityBot in robots.txt to appear in Perplexity search results

rules.description: CCBot (Common Crawl) is not blocked in robots.txt
rules.fix: Allow CCBot in robots.txt — Common Crawl data feeds many AI training datasets

rules.description: Google-Extended is not blocked in robots.txt
rules.fix: Allow Google-Extended in robots.txt to be included in Google's AI features (Gemini, AI Overviews)

rules.description: Bytespider (ByteDance/TikTok) status is intentional
rules.fix: Consider your Bytespider policy — blocking it prevents TikTok AI features but some sites prefer to block it

rules.description: Amazonbot status is intentional
rules.fix: Consider allowing Amazonbot for visibility in Alexa answers and Amazon search

rules.description: FacebookExternalHit status is intentional
rules.fix: Allow facebookexternalhit for proper link previews on Facebook and Meta platforms
